heading: Regional clean hydrogen hubs
label: 16161a Regional clean hydrogen hubs

children.heading: Definition of regional clean hydrogen hub
children.label: a Definition of regional clean hydrogen hub
children.text: In this section, the term “regional clean hydrogen hub” means a network of clean hydrogen producers, potential clean hydrogen consumers, and connective infrastructure located in close proximity.
demonstrably aid the achievement of the clean hydrogen production standard developed under ;section 16166(a) of this title
demonstrate the production, processing, delivery, storage, and end-use of clean hydrogen; and
can be developed into a national clean hydrogen network to facilitate a clean hydrogen economy.

children.heading: Selection of regional clean hydrogen hubs
children.label: c Selection of regional clean hydrogen hubs

children.heading: Solicitation of proposals
children.label: 1 Solicitation of proposals
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall solicit proposals for regional clean hydrogen hubs.November 15, 2021

children.heading: Selection of hubs
children.label: 2 Selection of hubs
children.text: Not later than 1 year after the deadline for the submission of proposals under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall select at least 4 regional clean hydrogen hubs to be developed under subsection (b).

children.label: i
children.text: at least 1 regional clean hydrogen hub shall demonstrate the production of clean hydrogen from fossil fuels;

children.label: ii
children.text: at least 1 regional clean hydrogen hub shall demonstrate the production of clean hydrogen from renewable energy; and

children.label: iii
children.text: at least 1 regional clean hydrogen hub shall demonstrate the production of clean hydrogen from nuclear energy.

children.label: i
children.text: at least 1 regional clean hydrogen hub shall demonstrate the end-use of clean hydrogen in the electric power generation sector;

children.label: ii
children.text: at least 1 regional clean hydrogen hub shall demonstrate the end-use of clean hydrogen in the industrial sector;

children.label: iii
children.text: at least 1 regional clean hydrogen hub shall demonstrate the end-use of clean hydrogen in the residential and commercial heating sector; and

children.label: iv
children.text: at least 1 regional clean hydrogen hub shall demonstrate the end-use of clean hydrogen in the transportation sector.

children.label: i
children.text: shall be located in a different region of the United States; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall use energy resources that are abundant in that region.

children.heading: Hubs in natural gas-producing regions
children.label: D Hubs in natural gas-producing regions
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, at least 2 regional clean hydrogen hubs shall be located in the regions of the United States with the greatest natural gas resources.

children.heading: Employment
children.label: E Employment
children.text: The Secretary shall give priority to regional clean hydrogen hubs that are likely to create opportunities for skilled training and long-term employment to the greatest number of residents of the region.

children.heading: Additional criteria
children.label: F Additional criteria
children.text: The Secretary may take into consideration other criteria that, in the judgment of the Secretary, are necessary or appropriate to carry out this subchapter1So in original. Probably should be followed by a period.1

children.heading: Funding of regional clean hydrogen hubs
children.label: 4 Funding of regional clean hydrogen hubs
children.text: The Secretary may make grants to each regional clean hydrogen hub selected under paragraph (2) to accelerate commercialization of, and demonstrate the production, processing, delivery, storage, and end-use of, clean hydrogen.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: d Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out this section $8,000,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2022 through 2026.
